generic_rules: Place contracts in component type annexes for properties of the component itself, and in implementation annexes for implementation-specific bindings.
Use the AADL model as the authoritative source for component structure (names, ports, types).
Focus on relating visible outputs to visible inputs when defining guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations when defining range assumptions/guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Avoid encoding AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) directly as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean or enumeration-like data ports into integers for range comparisons.
